pattern: Two pointers walking inward while skipping non-alphanumeric characters compare the string against its reverse in place, needing no cleaned copy.
hints: Only letters and digits count, and case is ignored, so non-alphanumeric characters should be passed over entirely.
Compare characters from both ends moving toward the middle rather than building a filtered string first.
Use left and right pointers: skip any end that is not alphanumeric, lowercase-compare the two, and fail the moment they differ.

edge_inputs.description: punctuation and spaces ignored, reads the same
input: A man, a plan, a canal: Panama

edge_inputs.description: alphanumeric content is not a palindrome
only a space leaves an empty alphanumeric string, trivially valid
mixed letters and digits forming a palindrome
case differences are normalised but order still matters

stress.description: long strings, half genuine palindromes and half random, with punctuation interleaved
stress.note: palindrome branch stays valid because reversed letters only differ by case, which the judge ignores
